description: Color harmony schemes and practical hue/saturation/lightness rules for pixel art. References based on Itten's color wheel + practical pixel-art deviations.

monochromatic.rule: Single hue, varied saturation/lightness. Most coherent, can be boring.

analogous.rule: 3 hues adjacent on the wheel (within ~30 degrees). Calming, naturalistic.

complementary.rule: 2 hues opposite on wheel. Maximum contrast. Use one as accent only - 80/20 split.
complementary.example: Red boss against teal-blue dungeon: viewer eye locks on boss

split_complementary.rule: 1 hue + the 2 hues adjacent to its complement. Less harsh than full complementary.
split_complementary.example: warm orange hero + cool blue-purple and blue-green enemies

triadic.rule: 3 hues evenly spaced (120 degrees apart). Vibrant but balanced.
triadic.warning: Easy to land in clown territory if all 3 are equal saturation. Knock 2 down to muted.

tetradic.rule: 4 hues forming rectangle on wheel (2 complementary pairs). Most variety, hardest to balance.
tetradic.tip: Pick one dominant hue, 2 supporting, 1 accent.

achromatic_with_accent.rule: Grayscale base + 1 saturated accent color used sparingly.
achromatic_with_accent.example: Steam Lords + a single blood-red accent for combat hits

hue_shift_table.description: Practical degree shifts for shadow/highlight in pixel art. NEVER pure lightness change.

saturation_curve.shadow: -15% to -25% saturation (going darker = less saturation up to mid, then can spike at deep shadow)
saturation_curve.highlight: +10% to +20% (highlights are MORE saturated than mid-tones, contrary to instinct)

temperature_rules.warm_recede_or_advance: Warm colors (red/orange/yellow) ADVANCE - they appear closer. Cool colors RECEDE - they appear further.

contrast_levels.value_contrast: Difference in lightness. Most important for readability.
contrast_levels.high_contrast: delta_L > 50% - silhouette reads at any zoom
contrast_levels.rule: Sprite-vs-background should be HIGH contrast in value. Within-sprite shading can be medium-low.
